acceptance_targets: [ch11-f001] Pass if the beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:264, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:265 contains no terms like "voice profile" or any comparable authorial-calibration language, and the revised sentence reads as Silas's lived observation of Damaris rather than commentary about character design. Fail if the paragraph still gestures outside the world of the novel.
[FBR-007] Pass if both cited lines are rewritten so the argument lands through scene texture and contradiction rather than an announced hinge; fail if either beat still uses explanatory cueing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:264, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:265 contains no terms like "voice profile" or any comparable authorial-calibration language, and the revised sentence reads as Silas's lived observation of Damaris rather than commentary about character design. Fail if the paragraph still gestures outside the world of the novel.
findings.problem: The sentence about what Damaris's "voice profile" would say is explicit process leakage. It imports out-of-world calibration language into close third person, breaks period immersion, and violates the constitution's ban on process leakage at a moment that should tighten pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the thought at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:264 so Silas reads Damaris's shift entirely through in-world perception: her body, timing, silence, or the way her newsroom performance drops off. Keep the beat that she stops narrating once the story becomes a wound she is inside, but remove any metatextual terminology from the surrounding beat anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:263, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:264, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:265.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines are rewritten so the argument lands through scene texture and contradiction rather than an announced hinge; fail if either beat still uses explanatory cueing.
findings.problem: Full-book explanatory hinge pattern ('There it was' / 'That landed') appears in 13 chapters. This chapter uses it twice, at 4039 and 4122, at exactly the moment the novel should trust Vero's history and Gideon's public logic to strike without interpretive scaffolding.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the hinge sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4039 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4122. In the first case, let Silas's failure of the 'rightful owner' instinct register through the mother tray and Vero's contempt. In the second, let Gideon's word 'ownership' and the room's sensory pressure do the damage without the narrator underlining it.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
